items.detected_how: pH was controlled by using defined buffer systems (citrate–phosphate, TEA–HCl, TRIS–HCl, glycine–NaOH) at specified pH values. No explicit mention of pH electrode calibration.
items.detected_when: pH was measured/set before the reaction by preparing the buffer at the desired pH. All components were present during the reaction.
items.special_treatment: For reduction assays, TEA–HCl buffer (50 mmol L⁻¹) with CaCl₂ (0.8 mmol L⁻¹) was used at pH 7.5. For oxidation assays, glycine–NaOH buffer (50 mmol L⁻¹) with CaCl₂ (0.8 mmol L⁻¹) at pH 10.5 was used. Buffers for pH stability studies included citrate–phosphate (pH 4.0–7.0), TEA–HCl (pH 7.0–8.0), TRIS–HCl (pH 7.0–8.5), glycine–NaOH (pH 8.5–12.0), all with CaCl₂ (0.8 mmol L⁻¹).

items.detected_how: pH was controlled by using defined buffer systems (citrate–phosphate, TEA–HCl, TRIS–HCl, glycine–NaOH) at specified pH values. No explicit mention of pH electrode calibration.
items.detected_when: pH was measured/set before the reaction by preparing the buffer at the desired pH. All components were present during the reaction.
items.special_treatment: For oxidation assays, glycine–NaOH buffer (50 mmol L⁻¹) with CaCl₂ (0.8 mmol L⁻¹) at pH 10.5 was used. For pH stability studies, a range of buffers and pH values were used as above.

items.detected_how: pH was controlled by using defined buffer systems (citrate–phosphate, TEA–HCl, TRIS–HCl, glycine–NaOH) at specified pH values. No explicit mention of pH electrode calibration.
items.detected_when: pH was measured/set before the reaction by preparing the buffer at the desired pH. All components were present during the reaction.
items.special_treatment: For kinetic measurements of cyclohexanol oxidation, TRIS–HCl buffer (50 mmol L⁻¹) with CaCl₂ (0.8 mmol L⁻¹) at pH 9.0 was used.
